questions.q: What is the primary function of directing in an organisation?
questions.answer: The primary function of directing in an organisation is to guide and instruct employees to achieve the organisational goals.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of effective communication? It _______.
Explain the importance of motivation in directing. Give two points.
questions.answer: Motivation is important in directing as it encourages employees to work towards achieving organisational goals. Two points of importance are: (i) it improves job satisfaction and (ii) it increases productivity.

questions.q: What is meant by span of control? State its significance.
questions.answer: Span of control refers to the number of subordinates that a manager can effectively supervise. Its significance lies in the fact that it helps in effective delegation of authority, communication and decision-making.

questions.q: Which leadership style is characterised by a democratic approach?
State the difference between autocratic and democratic leadership styles.
questions.answer: Autocratic leadership style is characterised by centralisation of authority, whereas democratic leadership style is characterised by decentralisation of authority and participation of subordinates in decision-making.

questions.q: What is the purpose of communication in directing?
questions.answer: The purpose of communication in directing is to convey instructions, policies and decisions to subordinates and to receive feedback from them.

questions.q: Explain the concept of leadership. Give two qualities of a good leader.
questions.answer: Leadership refers to the ability of an individual to influence and guide others to achieve a common goal. Two qualities of a good leader are: (i) initiative and (ii) integrity.

questions.q: Which of the following is a principle of effective communication? It should be _______.
What is meant by delegation of authority? State its benefits.
questions.answer: Delegation of authority refers to the process of assigning tasks and giving necessary powers to subordinates to achieve organisational goals. The benefits of delegation of authority are: (i) it relieves the manager of some of his workload, (ii) it develops the skills and abilities of subordinates, and (iii) it increases job satisfaction.
